title: Matrix DFS Traversal (Up, Right, Down, Left)
prompt:
Given a 2D array (matrix), return the values visited by a depth-first search starting at the top-left cell (0, 0).

Traversal rules:
- Visit a cell, then explore neighbors in this order: up, right, down, left.
- Do not visit a cell more than once.
- Stay within bounds.

Return an array of visited values in the order they are first visited.
If the matrix is empty, return an empty array.
constraintQuestions: What if the matrix is empty? (Return []).
Should we visit each cell at most once? (Yes.)
What directions are allowed? (Up, right, down, left only.)
Does traversal order matter? (Yes, use the given direction order.)

walkthrough.title: DFS with visited grid
walkthrough.body:
Create a visited matrix of the same size.
Use a recursive DFS to visit a cell, mark it visited, then explore neighbors in the specified order.
Collect values as you visit them.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(r*c) because each cell is visited once.
Space: O(r*c) for the visited grid and recursion stack.
